chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الدُّعَاءِ إِذَا خَرَجَ الْإِنْسَانُ مِنْ مَنْزِلِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 50 – The Supplication when the person exits from his house

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/323)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/323)
text: اسْتَأْذَنْتُ عَلَى أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) فَخَرَجَ إِلَيَّ وَ شَفَتَاهُ تَتَحَرَّكَانِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ فَقَالَ أَ فَطَنْتَ لِذَلِكَ يَا ثُمَالِيُّ قُلْتُ نَعَمْ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ قَالَ إِنِّي وَ اللَّهِ تَكَلَّمْتُ بِكَلَامٍ مَا تَكَلَّمَ بِهِ أَحَدٌ قَطُّ إِلَّا كَفَاهُ اللَّهُ مَا أَهَمَّهُ مِنْ أَمْرِ دُنْيَاهُ وَ آخِرَتِهِ
قَالَ قُلْتُ لَهُ أَخْبِرْنِي بِهِ قَالَ نَعَمْ مَنْ قَالَ حِينَ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ مَنْزِلِهِ.

بِسْمِ اللَّهِ حَسْبِيَ اللَّهُ تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اللَّهِ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ أُمُورِي كُلِّهَا وَ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ خِزْيِ الدُّنْيَا وَ عَذَابِ الْآخِرَةِ كَفَاهُ اللَّهُ مَا أَهَمَّهُ مِنْ أَمْرِ دُنْيَاهُ وَ آخِرَتِهِ

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Usman Bin Isa, from Abu Hamza who said,

‘I sought permission to (meet) Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, so he<sup>asws</sup> came out to me and his<sup>asws</sup> lips were moving. So I told him<sup>asws</sup>. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘You realised that O Sumaly?’ I said, ‘Yes, may I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>!’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘By Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! I<sup>asws</sup> spoke with a speech what no one would speak with it at all, except Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Suffice him for what worries him from the matters of his world and his Hereafter’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘I said to him<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup>, ‘Inform me of it’. He<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘The one who says when he goes out from his house,

‘In the Name of Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>! Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Suffices me. I rely upon Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>. O Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup>! I ask You<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> of the goodness in my affairs, all of them, and I seek Refuge with You<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> from the disgrace of the world and the Punishment of the Hereafter’, Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> will Suffice him for whateve worries him from the matters of his world and his Hereafter’.
en.sarwar: 3. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from abu Hamza who has said the following: “Once I asked permission to see abu Ja’far (a.s.) and he came out while his lips were moving. I then asked him about it and he said, ‘Did you, O al-Thumali, notice it?’ I said, ‘Yes, may Allah keep my soul in service for your cause, I did.’ The Imam said, ‘By Allah, I spoke the words that no one before had ever spoken, but that Allah had sufficed him in things important to him of worldly matters or of hereafter.’ I (the narrator) then said, ‘Please tell me about it.’ The Imam said, ‘Whoever when coming out of his house says, “In the name of Allah, enough for me is Allah, I place my trust in Allah. O Lord, I ask You to grant me all good in my affairs. I seek protection with You against the humiliation in this world and of torment in the next life,” Allah will suffice him in all that is important to him of worldly or of hereafter.’”
